home.heroTitle: Supporting Grieving Students Across New Jersey
home.heroSubtitle: A coalition of four family bereavement centers working together to bring grief education to every school in the state.
home.statDescription: school-aged children in the U.S. will experience the death of a parent or sibling before age 18
home.mandateDescription: P.L.2023, c.201 requires all New Jersey public schools to provide age-appropriate grief education. We're here to help schools meet this mandate with compassion and expertise.
home.coalitionDescription: The NJ Grief Collaborative brings together four leading family bereavement centers to support schools, families, and students across the state.
home.testimonialsDescription: Hear from educators, parents, and students about the impact of grief education.
home.ctaDescription: Whether you're a school professional, parent, or student, we have resources to support you.

about.title: About the NJ Grief Collaborative
about.subtitle: Four family bereavement centers united by a shared mission.
about.missionText: To ensure every student in New Jersey has access to compassionate, evidence-based grief education and support.
about.visionText: A New Jersey where no student grieves alone and every school is equipped to support bereaved youth.
about.whyText: When New Jersey passed P.L.2023, c.201, four family bereavement centers recognized an opportunity to combine decades of expertise to support schools statewide. Together, we bring diverse perspectives and deep community roots to this important work.

mandate.title: Understanding the Mandate
mandate.subtitle: Everything you need to know about P.L.2023, c.201
mandate.whatText: Signed into law in 2024, this landmark legislation requires all New Jersey public schools to provide age-appropriate grief education to students. It recognizes that grief is a universal experience and that schools play a critical role in supporting bereaved youth.

whoItems.schools: All New Jersey public school districts
whoItems.parents: Parents and guardians of school-aged children

isItems.education: Age-appropriate grief education integrated into existing curriculum
isItems.support: A framework for supporting bereaved students
isItems.awareness: A mandate for awareness and preparedness

isNotItems.therapy: A replacement for grief therapy or counseling
isNotItems.diagnostic: A tool for diagnosing mental health conditions

griefInformedSchools.title: Grief-Informed Schools
griefInformedSchools.subtitle: What does it mean to be a grief-informed school?
griefInformedSchools.introText: A grief-informed school recognizes that grief impacts learning and behavior, and creates an environment where bereaved students feel seen, supported, and safe.
griefInformedSchools.elementaryText: Age-appropriate lessons about feelings, loss, and healthy coping. Focus on normalizing grief as part of life.
griefInformedSchools.middleText: Deeper exploration of grief, identity, and peer support. Building emotional literacy and resilience.
griefInformedSchools.highText: Advanced discussions about grief, mortality, and supporting others. Leadership opportunities in peer support.

resources.subtitle: Tools and materials for school professionals, parents, and students.

training.subtitle: Building capacity for grief-informed schools across New Jersey.
training.introText: Our coalition offers a range of professional development opportunities designed to equip school professionals with the knowledge and skills to support grieving students.
training.requestText: Interested in bringing grief education training to your school or district? Contact us to discuss your needs.

home.heroTitle: Supporting NJ Schools and Students Through Grief and Loss
home.heroSubtitle: New Jersey's Commitment to Becoming Grief-Informed
home.heroDescription: In January 2024, New Jersey enacted P.L.2023, c.201 — requiring grief and loss education to be included in Health and Physical Education instruction for students in grades 8–12. The NJ Grief Collaborative is here to help schools, educators, families, and communities implement this mandate with confidence, compassion, and clarity.
home.whySubtitle: Grief Is More Prevalent Than You Might Think
home.statDescription: children in New Jersey will experience the death of a parent or sibling by the time they reach 18 years of age.
home.statMore: Many more experience other significant losses, such as divorce, incarceration, illness, community violence, displacement, natural disasters or identity-based loss.
home.griefStays: Grief does not stay at home. It shows up in classrooms, hallways, attendance records, behaviors, and academic performance.
home.mandateSubtitle: P.L.2023, c.201 requires grief and loss education within NJ Health and Physical Education standards for grades 8–12.
home.mandateBullet1: Normalizes grief as a human experience
home.mandateBullet2: Provides students with developmentally appropriate coping tools
home.mandateBullet3: Expands the conversation to include many forms of loss
home.mandateDisclaimer: This mandate does not require personal disclosure, therapy, or teachers to become counselors.
home.coalitionSubtitle: We are a coalition of New Jersey's leading family bereavement centers.
home.coalitionCollaboration: Our organizations collaborate on training, materials, consultation, and community response to ensure schools feel supported — not overwhelmed.
home.schoolsTrait1: Recognizes that loss impacts learning and creates systems that support students throughout their school experience
home.schoolsTrait2: Prepares educators with professional development to support students and colleagues
home.schoolsTrait3: Integrates grief education into the school day thoughtfully and with sensitivity without requiring self-disclosure from students
home.schoolsTrait4: Provides pathways to additional support when needed
home.schoolsQuote: Schools who are grief-informed can create more supportive learning environments for students and the school community as a whole. When grief is addressed proactively, it can help grieving students become more equipped to concentrate, and in turn, be more prepared for learning.
home.voicesSubtitle: Short reflections from students, educators, and caregivers.
home.ctaTitle: Ready to Take the Next Step?
home.ctaSubtitle: Whether you are a school leader, educator, policymaker, or caregiver, we are here to help.
home.ctaSchoolsDesc: Professional development, curriculum partners, and implementation guidance.
home.ctaFamiliesDesc: Resources to support children and teens who are grieving.
home.ctaLegislatorsDesc: Evidence-based insight into why grief education strengthens schools.
home.griefNotStaysHome: Grief does not stay at home
home.fiveCenters: Five leading family bereavement centers, one mission.
home.njCommitment: New Jersey's Commitment to Becoming Grief-Informed
home.voice1: This is one of the best things I've done in my life. I feel less alone and more connected to everyone in the school.
home.voice2: I really enjoyed everything about the presentation. I am excited to learn and teach the lesson to my students in the future.
home.voice3: An incredible refuge for comfort, support, and sense of belonging for my family during the hardest time in our life. Thank you for everything you do!
